statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{m-4}+\frac{2}{m-4}}}{{5-\frac{1}{m+4}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{m-4}+\frac{2}{m-4}} and {5-\frac{1}{m+4}}, which is {\left(m+4\right)\mathopen{}\left(m-4\right)}.
Since m+4 was canceled, m \ne -4 is a lost domain condition.
Although m-4 was also canceled, m \ne 4 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
